header.title: Our Story
header.description: Bridging the authentic, raw, and vibrant flavors of Kolkata street lanes with high-quality, modern fast-food dining.

story.title: A Bridge Over Flavors
paragraphs: Founded in 2024 by two childhood friends from North Kolkata, Kolkata-Howrah Bridge Special was born out of a simple longing: to find authentic Kolkata street food that carried the real wok smoke and tang of the street side.

Named after the iconic cantilever Kolkata-Howrah Bridge that spans the Hooghly River, our joint serves as a culinary link. We connect traditional street griddling, roaring Indo-Chinese wok techniques, and starter frying to create high-quality comfort food.
We don't compromise on heritage. Our spice blends are hand-pounded weekly, and our ingredients are selected to give that street-side punch you won't find anywhere else.

quote.text: Food in Kolkata is not just sustenance; it is a conversation, an emotion, and a legacy. We wanted to build a bridge that carries that legacy forward.
quote.author: Joydeep & Amit, Founders

pillarsSection.title: Our Three Pillars
pillarsSection.subtitle: What makes our kitchen distinct and our menu special.

items.title: No Shortcuts
items.text: We never use pre-packaged doughs or liquid smoke. Everything from our green chutneys to our paneer and chicken marinates is prepared from scratch daily in our kitchen.

items.title: Tangra Chinatown Woks
items.text: Our street-style noodles and fried rice are tossed at blistering temperatures in heavy carbon-steel woks for that signature smoky wok-hei flavor.

items.title: Sunday Specialty
items.text: We are dedicated to bringing back lost street recipes. Our Sunday special Alukatta is prepared fresh on Sunday evenings using authentic ingredients.

teamSection.title: Our Culinary Masters
teamSection.subtitle: Meet the skilled hands and experienced palates behind our popular specialties.

members.name: Chef Biswajit Das
members.description: Over 18 years of experience griddling and frying street-style starters in Kolkata's historic Park Street cabinets.

members.name: Arindam Sinha
members.description: Trained in Kolkata's Tangra Chinatown district, specializing in roaring wok mechanics and dark-soy street Hakka noodle seasoning.

members.name: Joydeep Dey
members.description: Oversees sourcing fresh mint, street spices, and potatoes. Architect of the Sunday Special Alukatta.
